properties.name: File size limit
properties.statement: Recording.size_bytes MUST be <= 524288000 bytes (500 MB). Uploads exceeding the threshold are rejected before storage.
properties.rationale: Prevents runaway storage costs and ASR queue saturation from very large files at MVP scale.

properties.name: Allowed file formats
properties.statement: Recording.mime_type MUST be one of {video/mp4, video/x-matroska, video/quicktime} (MP4, MKV, MOV). Other types are rejected.
properties.rationale: ASR pipeline supports audio extraction only from these container formats; accepting others causes silent failures.

properties.assumption_note: Validation method TBD (FFprobe header check or short-duration probe). Full frame-level integrity not guaranteed at MVP.
properties.name: File integrity
properties.statement: The uploaded file MUST be a valid, non-corrupt video container from which audio can be extracted. Corrupt or unplayable files are rejected during upload acceptance.
properties.rationale: Corrupt files cause opaque failures deep in the ASR pipeline; early validation gives a clearer user-facing error.

properties.name: Supported languages
properties.statement: Meeting.language MUST be one of {ru, en, auto}. Other values are rejected at upload.
properties.rationale: MVP supports RU and EN only; auto-detect delegated to ASR. Broader languages out of scope.

properties.name: Auto-detect language fallback
properties.statement: If Meeting.language = auto, the ASR engine MUST detect the language and the detected value is recorded as Transcript.language. The Meeting.language record stays as auto.
properties.rationale: Auto-detection should not silently overwrite user input; Transcript.language records ground truth.

properties.name: One TranscriptionJob per Recording
properties.statement: For each Recording there MUST be exactly one TranscriptionJob (1:1 relationship). Re-transcription is treated as a new Recording or explicit reprocess (out of MVP scope).
properties.rationale: Simplifies status tracking and avoids divergent transcripts.

properties.name: Auto-trigger protocol generation
properties.statement: When TranscriptionJob.status transitions to completed, a ProtocolGenerationJob MUST be created automatically for the resulting Transcript.
properties.rationale: User does not need to manually initiate protocol generation; this is part of the end-to-end pipeline.

properties.name: Meeting status mirrors pipeline
properties.statement: Meeting.status MUST reflect the current pipeline stage and follow the allowed lifecycle (uploading -> transcribing -> transcript_ready -> protocol_generating -> protocol_ready -> edited | failed).
properties.rationale: The Meeting is the single authoritative status surface for the user; jobs carry internal status separately.

properties.name: Job terminal states are immutable
properties.statement: Once TranscriptionJob.status or ProtocolGenerationJob.status reaches {completed, failed}, it MUST NOT change.
properties.rationale: Terminal states are an audit anchor; mutating them would invalidate downstream artifacts and event records.

properties.name: Failure recorded with reason
properties.statement: If a job status is failed, error_reason MUST be non-null and human-readable so the UI can surface it to the user.
properties.rationale: Silent failures degrade trust and make support diagnostics impossible.
